name: Shekhinah (Divine Presence)
shortDescription: The Shekhinah is the dwelling or settling of the divine presence of God. It represents the manifestation of God's glory and presence in the world, particularly in places of worship or among the righteous.
description: The Shekhinah (Hebrew: שְׁכִינָה‎) is a concept in Jewish theology that refers to the dwelling or settling of the divine presence of God. It is not a separate entity from God but rather a manifestation of God's glory and immanence in the world. The term Shekhinah does not appear explicitly in the Hebrew Bible but is developed in Rabbinic literature, particularly in the Talmud and Kabbalah. The Shekhinah is often associated with the Tabernacle and later the Temple in Jerusalem, where it was believed that God's presence resided among the Israelites. The Shekhinah is also associated with moments of divine revelation, such as the giving of the Torah at Mount Sinai. In Kabbalah, the Shekhinah is understood as the feminine aspect of God and is seen as the source of divine compassion and nurturing. The Shekhinah is believed to accompany the Jewish people in exile and to be present wherever they gather to pray and study Torah. The concept of the Shekhinah emphasizes the idea that God is not distant or detached from the world but is actively involved in human affairs. The association of the Shekhinah with the Ark of the Covenant highlights its role as a tangible manifestation of God's covenant with the Israelites. The Shekhinah's presence also provides comfort and guidance to individuals seeking spiritual connection and understanding. The idea that the Shekhinah is always present, even in times of difficulty, offers hope and reassurance to believers.

sources.title: The Zohar
sources.author: Various Authors (attributed to Shimon bar Yochai)
